summary: Rectal anastomosis during gynecologic debulking surgery may be safely performed without a diverting stoma in cases involving minimal rectal injury or specific surgical indications like transanal excision or drain placement for suprapubic infections. Evidence from existing literature shows favorable outcomes with no significant adverse events when appropriate patient selection criteria are met.
